global.motto: Rediscover Cameroon, one Waka at a Time

hero.pill: 2 trips open · Le Point Maka 20 June & Kribi 16–19 July
hero.subtitle: Small-group journeys to the coastlines, rainforests, highlands and summits most travellers never see — led by locals who call them home.

intro.heading: "Waka" means to walk, to go, to set off. We turn the most beautiful corners of Cameroon into journeys you'll never forget — without the stress of planning them yourself.
intro.body: From the black-sand beaches of Kribi to the summit of Mount Cameroon, we handle the logistics, the guides and the details — so all you carry is your camera and your sense of wonder.
intro.quote: We don't show you Cameroon. We introduce you to it.

why.heading: Travel that feels personal, run by people who care
why.body1: WakaWithUS began in 2024, when two friends from Buea — Ekema and Onell — set off to visit the Bimbia Slave Trade Center. That one trip turned into a movement: helping people rediscover Cameroon, one Waka at a time.
why.body2: We're still small, still local, and we still plan every journey like it's for a friend. You bring the curiosity — we handle everything else.

features.title: Local expert guides
features.text: Every journey is led by Cameroonians who grew up on these trails and speak the local languages.

features.title: All-inclusive, no surprises
features.text: Transport, lodging, meals and entry fees are bundled into one clear price. No hidden costs.

features.title: Small groups only
features.text: We cap every trip so you get real connection, flexibility and access — never a tourist convoy.

features.title: Safe & fully supported
features.text: Risk-assessed routes, insured transport and 24/7 support before, during and after your trip.

howItWorks.heading: From first click to first adventure
howItWorks.body: Booking with us takes minutes, not days. No endless email chains — just a quick chat and you're on your way.

steps.title: Browse
steps.text: Explore our upcoming journeys and find the one that calls to you.

steps.title: Reach out
steps.text: Message us on WhatsApp or the contact form — we reply within hours.

steps.title: Reserve
steps.text: Secure your place with a simple deposit and get your full itinerary.

steps.title: Waka!
steps.text: Show up with your camera. We take care of every other detail.

destinations.heading: Four worlds, one country
destinations.note: Cameroon is called "Africa in miniature" for a reason. Hover a region to look closer.

regions.name: The Coast
regions.blurb: Black-sand beaches where waterfalls pour into the Atlantic.

regions.name: The Highlands
regions.blurb: Cool air, ancient kingdoms and endless rolling green.

regions.name: The Rainforest
regions.blurb: Hundred-million-year-old canopy, gorillas and forest elephants.

regions.name: The Summits
regions.blurb: West Africa's highest peak — 4,095m of raw volcanic power.

trips.title: Le Point Maka — The Forgotten Land
trips.meetingPoint: Buea — exact point shared on booking
trips.highlight: Step into history at the abandoned Maka site — the forgotten land
trips.description: A one-day mini adventure into history and nature. Tour the abandoned Maka site, cool off with a stream swim and roasted corn, share a group dinner, then end the day with a cinema night on a rooftop. Limited to 15 adventurers — all costs included.

trips.title: Kribi — A Coastal Escape
trips.dressCode: Simple African wear for the barbecue (e.g. Ankara)
trips.highlight: Black-sand beaches where the Lobé Falls pour straight into the Atlantic
trips.description: Four sun-soaked days on Cameroon's most beautiful coastline — the rare Lobé waterfalls that fall into the sea, a Pygmy village, boat and canoe rides, a cocktail pool party and a barbecue night. Lodging, transport and a pro photo & drone team all included.

schedule.activity: Arrival in Kribi & check-in (4-star guest house)
Lobé Waterfall — the falls that meet the sea
Boat & canoe rides along the coast
Final beach time, drone & group photos

services.title: General Trips
services.text: Scheduled small-group journeys across Cameroon's coast, highlands and forests. All-inclusive, social and completely stress-free — just show up and explore.

services.title: Private Trip
services.text: A bespoke journey built entirely around you — your dates, your pace, your people. Perfect for couples, families and groups who want it their way.

services.title: Trip Consultation
services.text: Planning your own adventure? Book a session and tap our local knowledge — routes, budgets, safety and the logistics most travellers miss.

services.title: Trip Negotiation
services.text: We use our local relationships to negotiate hotels, transport and activities on your behalf — so you always pay fair, local prices, never the tourist markup.

services.title: Fitness & Networking
services.text: Hikes, beach workouts and outdoor meet-ups that keep you active while you build real connections with like-minded explorers.

services.title: Nightlife Adventure
services.text: Guided nights out — the best bars, live music and street food, with the right crowd and a local who keeps the whole evening safe and smooth.

about.heading: We are the Walkers
about.storyHeading: It started with one trip in 2024
story: In 2024, two friends from Buea — Ekema and Onell — set off to visit the Bimbia Slave Trade Center. What began as a simple day out became something bigger.
Standing in that historic place, they realised how much of Cameroon's beauty and history sits right on our doorstep — unseen by the very people who live closest to it.

So they started walking. "Waka" — to go, to move, to explore. They invited friends, then friends of friends, and WakaWithUS grew into a community helping people rediscover Cameroon, one Waka at a time.

founders.name: Ekema
founders.bio: A Buea local with a love for the road and a gift for bringing people together.

founders.name: Onell
founders.bio: The planner of the pair — turning spontaneous ideas into journeys that actually work.

timeline.title: The first Waka
timeline.desc: Ekema and Onell leave Buea for the Bimbia Slave Trade Center — the trip that started it all.

timeline.title: Friends join in
timeline.desc: Word spreads. Weekend trips with friends quickly turn into organised group journeys.

timeline.title: A growing community
timeline.desc: More walkers, more destinations across the South West and beyond.

timeline.title: WakaWithUS today
timeline.desc: A fresh brand, a new home online, and our upcoming trips this June and July.

values.title: Authenticity
values.desc: Real places, real people, real Cameroon — never a manufactured tourist trap.

values.title: Community
values.desc: Every trip is built on friendship. You arrive as a guest and leave as family.

values.title: Safety first
values.desc: Risk-assessed routes, trusted transport and someone with you the whole way.

values.title: Local always
values.desc: We hire local, buy local and travel light — leaving each place better than we found it.

testimonials.name: Amara Nkeng
testimonials.review: Absolutely breathtaking! WakaWithUS made every detail seamless — from the transport to the beachside dinners. Kribi is magical and this team made it even more so.

testimonials.name: Jean-Baptiste Fouda
testimonials.review: As a Cameroonian living in France, this trip reconnected me with my roots in the most profound way. The cultural visits, the food, the people — I came back a different person.

testimonials.name: Sophie Laurent
testimonials.review: I've trekked in the Alps and the Himalayas, but something about Mount Cameroon was different. Raw, powerful, humbling. The WakaWithUS guides were exceptional.

testimonials.name: Nsimba Patrice
testimonials.review: Perfectly organised, no stress, just pure joy. Already booked my next trip!

faq.a: Everyone — solo travellers, couples, families and groups of friends. Most of our walkers are Cameroonians rediscovering their own country, plus a growing community of visitors from abroad. Our small-group format means you'll always feel welcome.
Transport, accommodation, meals, entry fees and your local guide are all bundled into one clear price. The only things you'll spend extra on are souvenirs and personal items. No hidden costs, ever.

faq.q: How do I book and pay?
faq.a: Message us on WhatsApp or via the contact form. We'll confirm availability, send your full itinerary, and secure your spot with a deposit. The balance is due before departure — we'll guide you through every step.
Safety is our first priority. Every route is risk-assessed, our transport is insured, our guides are trained, and we provide 24/7 support before, during and after your journey.

faq.q: Can you organise private or corporate trips?
faq.a: Absolutely. Alongside our scheduled group departures, we craft bespoke private journeys and corporate retreats. Tell us your dates, group size and dream destinations and we'll build the perfect package.
